title: olearia phlogopappa subsp. gunniana
intro: Olearia phlogopappa subsp. gunniana, also known as bushy daisy or native daisy, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Olearia phlogopappa subsp. gunniana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as its use as a topical treatment for wounds and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it can also be used as a poultice to aid in the healing process.
